reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Hebrew, preserving code blocks, frontmatter, etc. Must adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original frontmatter has relative paths: social_image: desktop-social.webp, cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp, cover_mobile: ./square-200.webp, cover_icon: ./square-200.webp. Need to prefix with ../ for locale folder. So change to ../desktop-social.webp? Actually rule: "ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`." So for frontmatter values that are paths, we need to add ../ before them. So social_image: ../desktop-social.webp, cover_full_width: ../hero-wide.webp (remove ./?), but we need to keep same relative path style, just prefix with ../. So if original "./hero-wide.webp", prefix with ../ gives ".././hero-wide.webp"? Probably just "../hero-wide.webp". We'll use "../hero-wide.webp". Similarly cover_mobile: "../square-200.webp". cover_icon: same.

Also there is an image in markdown: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) => need to prefix with ../: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg). Another image later: ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) => change to ../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp.

Also there is a blockquote with class "inset". Keep as is.

Need to translate prose to Hebrew, preserving headings levels. Keep code blocks unchanged.

Also need to translate frontmatter values: title, subTitle, maybe cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" => Hebrew translation: "הגיע הזמן למחרוזות חיבור llm://". subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" => "פשטו את תצורת המודל והספק עם כתובות `llm://`". language field not present; we need to add language: "Hebrew"? The spec: translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit. If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Hebrew". There's no language field currently, but we could add? It says if a language field exists, set it. Not required to add. Probably we keep as is, not add language.

We must keep other metadata unchanged.

Now translate body.

Let's translate each paragraph.

First blockquote: "**Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/)." => Hebrew: "**עדכון:** מאמר זה הוביל ל[טיוטת אינטרנט עבור סכמת ה-`llm://` URI](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/)." Keep link unchanged.

Next paragraph: "Remember the bad old days when connecting to a database meant juggling a miscellaneous grab-bag of environment variables?" => Hebrew: "זוכרים את הימים הקשים כשהתחברות למסד נתונים דרשה ניהול של חבילה מבולגנת של משתני סביבה?" Keep tone.

Next: "It was a tower of delicate config. `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`... or wait, was it `DB_USERNAME`? Is it `DB_PASS` or `DB_PWD`? Do I need the `PG_*` prefixes this time? And where the hell does the timeout setting go?" => Hebrew translation.

Continue.

Proceed.

Also note footnote marker ¹ after URL. Keep.
